skill.name: bolder
skill.description: Amplify safe or boring designs to make them more visually interesting and stimulating. Increases impact while maintaining usability.
skill.summary: Transform generic, safe designs into bold, memorable experiences through intentional visual amplification
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 21 false positives related to the word 'bolder' being misidentified as 'blocker' and incorrectly categorized as weak cryptography. After manual review, the skill contains only instructional design documentation with no executable code, no network access, no file operations, and no security risks. All findings are dismissed as pattern-matching errors in a text-only design guidance skill.

content.user_title: Transform Boring Designs Into Bold Experiences
content.value_statement: Stop settling for generic, forgettable interfaces. This skill helps Claude create visually striking designs that captivate users while maintaining usability and accessibility standards.
actual_capabilities: Amplify visual impact through typography scale, weight contrast, and font selection
Intensify color palettes with vibrant saturation and unexpected combinations
Create spatial drama with extreme scale jumps and asymmetric layouts
Add visual effects including shadows, textures, and background treatments
Implement motion and animation with entrance choreography and micro-interactions
Verify bold designs meet accessibility, performance, and usability standards
limitations: Requires frontend-design skill dependency for full context gathering protocol
Cannot modify brand guidelines or override strict accessibility requirements
May require manual iteration to achieve the right level of boldness for specific brands
Effectiveness depends on existing design context and constraints in the codebase

use_cases.title: Revamp Generic Landing Pages
use_cases.description: Transform cookie-cutter landing pages with system fonts and basic layouts into memorable brand experiences that drive conversions through distinctive visual design.

use_cases.title: Elevate Dashboard Interfaces
use_cases.description: Add visual personality to data-heavy dashboards without sacrificing clarity, making complex information more engaging through strategic typography and color choices.
use_cases.target_user: B2B SaaS developers and UX designers

use_cases.title: Strengthen Brand Identity
use_cases.description: Amplify brand presence across applications by replacing generic design patterns with bold, cohesive visual language that reinforces brand personality.
use_cases.target_user: Design systems teams and brand designers

prompt_templates.title: Make My Hero Section Bolder
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the bolder skill to amplify my landing page hero section. Focus on creating a dramatic focal point with the headline and call-to-action, using extreme typography scale and a striking color treatment.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to transform a generic hero section into something attention-grabbing

prompt_templates.title: Add Visual Drama to Dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply bolder principles to my dashboard interface. Increase visual hierarchy through dramatic scale differences between metrics, headers, and data. Keep data readable but make the interface more engaging.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a functional but boring dashboard that needs more visual personality

prompt_templates.title: Break the Grid Layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Use bolder to redesign my product grid layout. Introduce asymmetric arrangements, overlap elements for depth, and create visual tension that guides attention to key products.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to escape standard grid layouts for something more dynamic

prompt_templates.title: Amplify Typography System
prompt_templates.prompt: Run bolder on my typography system. Replace generic fonts with distinctive choices, create extreme scale contrast (3x-5x differences), and pair heavy weights with light weights for dramatic impact.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to transform their type hierarchy from standard to striking

output_examples.input: My landing page hero looks generic with a centered headline and small CTA button. Make it bolder.
output_examples.output: I've amplified your hero section with a 5x larger headline using a heavy-weight display font, moved the CTA to an asymmetric position with a vibrant accent color, added a subtle mesh gradient background in deep indigo, and implemented a staggered entrance animation. The headline now breaks the container boundary for added drama.

output_examples.input: This dashboard feels boring with all metrics displayed the same way. Apply bolder principles.
output_examples.output: I've created dramatic hierarchy: key KPIs are 4x larger with bold accent colors, secondary metrics use a condensed font at medium size, and tertiary data appears in light weight. Added a monospace accent font for metric labels and introduced tinted grays instead of neutral grays for richer visual depth.

output_examples.input: Our pricing page looks like every other SaaS pricing page. Make it stand out.
output_examples.output: I've broken symmetry with an asymmetric layout where the featured plan escapes its card boundary, introduced a bold color that dominates 60% of the design, added thick decorative borders to the featured tier, and implemented scroll-triggered animations that reveal pricing tiers sequentially for memorable impact.

best_practices: Always run the frontend-design skill first to gather design context and understand anti-patterns
Start with ONE focal point - making everything bold means nothing stands out
Use the 3x-5x scale rule for typography - dramatic jumps create visual interest
Test bold designs against accessibility standards and verify readability isn't sacrificed
Avoid AI slop patterns like generic purple-blue gradients and glassmorphism - bold means distinctive, not derivative
anti_patterns: Don't add random effects without purpose - chaos is not bold design
Never sacrifice body text readability for dramatic headlines - accessibility remains mandatory
Avoid copying trendy aesthetics blindly - bold means unique to the brand, not following design fads
Don't overwhelm with motion and animation - too much animation creates fatigue and distracts from content

faq.question: Does bolder work with strict brand guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill emphasizes understanding constraints and brand personality before amplifying. Bold design can be achieved within guidelines through dramatic scale, strategic color use, and intentional typography choices that align with brand identity.

faq.question: Will bold designs hurt accessibility?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly maintains WCAG accessibility standards. Bold design increases contrast and hierarchy, which actually improves readability when done correctly. Body text remains legible while visual impact increases.

faq.question: Can I use bolder for data-heavy dashboards?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Bolder principles work well for dashboards by creating clear visual hierarchy. Large KPIs stand out while secondary data recedes, making complex information easier to scan without overwhelming users.

faq.question: Does bolder require CSS expertise?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides design guidance that Claude translates into CSS implementation. You describe what needs amplification and Claude generates the appropriate styles, from typography scale to animation timing.

faq.question: How do I avoid the AI slop trap mentioned in the skill?
faq.answer: The skill warns against generic patterns like cyan/purple gradients, glassmorphism, and neon accents. Instead, focus on distinctive choices specific to your brand: unexpected color combinations, dramatic scale, and unique typography that doesn't look like every AI-generated design.

faq.question: What if my design becomes too bold?
faq.answer: Start with conservative amplification and iterate. The skill recommends asking for clarification if brand personality or risk tolerance is unclear. You can always dial back individual elements like color saturation or animation timing.
